name: design-rules
title: Design rules (law + craft + usage)
description: The files that govern designing with Pucar: design-guidelines.md (the law ‚Äî teal rationing, sentence case, AA, colour semantics, motion), screen-craft.md (the craft ‚Äî spacing ladder, scaffolds, hierarchy, UX patterns, states, review pass), component-usage.md (when and how to use each component, with decision trees), and a portable agent skill (.claude/skills/pucar-design) so AI sessions in the consumer repo inherit the judgment automatically. Release 2026-07-21.

files.content:
# Designing with Pucar ¬∑ ON Court

> The **design-language skill** for the Pucar design system. Read this before designing or generating any screen.
> This is where design *policy* lives ‚Äî edit it freely as taste evolves. The token/component **reference** is generated separately (see `llms.txt`); this file is the human-authored intent that `llms.txt`, `CLAUDE.md`, Figma and Pencil all point to.

---

## 0 ¬∑ North star ‚Äî the feel

Calm, dignified **public service**. A courts product used by citizens under stress and by staff all day. It must feel **friendly, not intimidating**, trustworthy, and quiet ‚Äî *premium through restraint, never flashy*. When unsure, choose the calmer, more legible, more forgiving option. "Airy and a little apologetic."

---

## 1 ¬∑ Non-negotiable rules

### Typography
- **Sentence case only.** Never ALL-CAPS or Title Case ‚Äî including eyebrow/section labels, buttons, table headers, nav. **Exceptions:** proper nouns (ON Court, Pucar) and established legal abbreviations (CNR, FIR, IPC, S.138). *Why: caps shout, which intimidates; and Indic scripts have no case, so caps don't translate.*
- Body defaults to **16px** (reading + UI). Use 14 (`body-compact`) only for dense, staff-only data.
- **No thin text.** Honour the weight ladder (caption‚Üí500, body‚Üí400/500/600, titles‚Üí600). Sub-400 never ships.
- Hierarchy comes from **weight + `neutral-11`**, not from colour.

### Colour
- **Brand teal is rationed** ‚Äî primary action, active state, focus. Never decoration.
- **Status colours mean status** (success / warning / info / destructive), never styling.
- **Each status hue has exactly three treatments** (ruling 2026-07-21): the **solid** (`success` + `success-foreground`) ‚Äî a fill for the one strong action per view; the **tint pair** (`success-muted` + `success-muted-foreground`) ‚Äî chips, callouts, flagged rows; the **ink** (`success-ink`) ‚Äî status-coloured text and icons on neutral surfaces (page, card, wells). The solid is never a text colour; the ink is never a fill. Inks are engineered to clear 4.5:1 on the page *and* inside `surface-sunken` (amber-11 and blue-11 both missed, so `warning-ink`/`info-ink` are tuned ‚Äî the amber stays amber rather than going brown-12).
- **No alpha status/brand fills.** A translucent tint's contrast depends on whatever sits underneath (`bg-destructive/10` measured 4.44:1 on `accent` ‚Äî under the floor). Every tint that carries text is an opaque, gate-verified pair. Alpha remains legal in exactly three places: focus rings, shadows, and washes over *content that must stay readable through them* (document-annotation highlights, scrims).
- **Data-viz uses the categorical palette** (`chart-1‚Ä¶5`) ‚Äî chosen for mutual distinction, *not* status meaning.
- Neutrals carry structure (surfaces, borders, text).
- **Greys form one monotone fill ladder, each step one job** (re-ruled 2026-07-21 ‚Äî the old "interactive stays lighter than structural" wording was internally false): `muted`/`sidebar` (2 ‚Äî quiet washes) ‚Üí `surface-sunken` (tuned 2¬Ω ‚Äî nested wells, separating on a card **with no border**) ‚Üí `accent` (3 ‚Äî hover of white/transparent-rest controls, and the rest fill of `secondary`) ‚Üí `accent-strong` (4 ‚Äî one step further: hover of grey-rest controls, pressed toggles, expanded triggers) ‚Üí `track` (5 ‚Äî recessed control tracks, the darkest fill, so a white/teal active pops) ‚Üí borders (5/7) ‚Üí `input` (9). Interaction always moves **one step down the ladder** ‚Äî a control never hovers to its own rest colour (the bug that made secondary-button hovers invisible). Never pick a raw `neutral-N` for any of these jobs.
- **Depth is fill, not repeated borders.** The outer card keeps the one hairline; nested wells use `surface-sunken` with **no border of their own** ‚Äî a border on a nested item reads as box-in-box. Exception: small chips/thumbnails are defined *by* a border. **Never pick a raw `neutral-N` for any surface/track/accent** ‚Äî change the one token and it propagates everywhere.
- **Never rely on colour alone.** Pair it with an icon, label, or arrow (e.g. ‚Üë/‚Üì on deltas). *Colour-blind and bad-monitor safe.*

### Accessibility ‚Äî the floor, not the aspiration
- **WCAG 2.2 AA:** text ‚â• 4.5:1, large text / UI boundaries ‚â• 3:1. **Verify, don't eyeball.**
- Interactive states must stay **perceptible** ‚Äî hover/selection use `accent` (a calibrated light neutral, deliberately calm) or a brand tint, never pure page-white. The AA gate checks `accent-foreground` on `accent` in both themes.
- Focus is always visible (the teal ring). Don't remove it.
- Touch targets ‚â• **40px** on citizen-facing surfaces.

### Spacing & density
- **Generous by default.** Whitespace is a feature, not waste ‚Äî it lowers cognitive load and reads as calm and premium.
- 4px grid, 8px rhythm; the 2px nudge is optical-only, never layout.
- Earn density only where trained staff live in tables ‚Äî via line-height and padding, never by shrinking type or targets.

### Elevation & shape
- Depth is **semantic**: flat (page), raised (cards, optional), overlay (popovers), modal (dialogs). No decorative shadows.
- One system radius (10px) and its derived scale. No loose values.
- **Radius is assigned by role, not by eye** (ruling 2026-07-21): container (card, dialog, bordered region) ‚Üí `xl` ¬∑ control (button, field, track) ‚Üí `lg` ¬∑ inset item (menu row, list row, inner button) ‚Üí `md`/`sm` ¬∑ micro-control (checkbox) ‚Üí derived `sm` ¬∑ **chip ‚Üí `full`, always**. Bare `rounded` (a fixed 4px that ignores the knob) is illegal. Siblings at the same anatomy level share one radius.

### Motion
- **Motion is feedback for something the user did ‚Äî never ambient, never decorative.** A calm public instrument doesn't move on its own.
- Two duration bands (Material 3's tokens, cut to our register): **micro feedback** (hover, focus, pressed) 100‚Äì150ms ¬∑ **surface transitions** (overlays opening, sections expanding) 200‚Äì250ms. **Nothing over 300ms, ever.**
- **Ease-out entering, ease-in exiting** (decelerate into view, accelerate out) ‚Äî things arrive gently and leave quickly.
- **`prefers-reduced-motion` collapses everything to instant state changes** ‚Äî enforced globally in `globals.css` (`@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)`), so no component can forget. State changes still happen; only the choreography disappears (WCAG 2.3.3).

### Control metrics
- **Every field-like control defaults to 40px tall** ‚Äî input, select, composed field (input-group), OTP slot, command input, tabs track. The size ladder is 32 / 36 / **40** / 44; smaller sizes are *opt-in* for staff-dense surfaces, never a primitive's default. Citizen-facing primary flows step up to 44 (`lg`).
- **Container padding is one number: 24px** (`p-6`) ‚Äî card, dialog, sheet, drawer, nested well. The `sm` card variant (staff-dense) uses 16. Nothing else.
- **There is exactly one chip: `Badge`.** 24px tall, pill-shaped, caption type. Status variants (`success/warning/info/destructive`) use the opaque muted token pairs ‚Äî never alpha fills, whose contrast shifts with the backdrop (`bg-destructive/10` measured 4.44:1 on `accent` ‚Äî below AA).

---

## 2 ¬∑ Information design
- **One primary line per row**; secondary info muted beneath it. *(A hearing row = parties in medium weight; CNR muted-mono below.)*
- Prefer **dividers over density**; keep table headers quiet (`muted-foreground`) so the *data* reads first.
- Numbers and IDs are tabular; CNRs are monospace.
- **Progressive disclosure** ‚Äî show the essential, link to the rest ("4 of 27 shown ‚Üí View cause list").
- One obvious primary action per view. Reduce the number of choices on screen.

## 3 ¬∑ Responsiveness
- **Mobile-first.** Sidebar collapses to a drawer; multi-column grids stack; touch targets stay ‚â• 40px.
- Comfortable reading max-widths; tables scroll horizontally rather than crush.
- Test at **375 / 768 / 1280**.

## 4 ¬∑ Robustness ‚Äî the Pucar stress tests
- **Bad monitor:** if a state depends on a near-white grey, it fails. Calibrated greys / brand tints for anything interactive.
- **Low network:** system-font stack (zero download); avoid heavy assets.
- **Indic scripts:** sentence case; ensure Malayalam renders (OS fallback / bundled Noto Sans Malayalam).

## 5 ¬∑ Choosing components
- Primary action ‚Üí `Button` default (teal). Secondary ‚Üí `outline` / `secondary`. Tertiary ‚Üí `ghost`.
- **Solid coloured action buttons** (`success` / `warning` / `info` / `destructive-solid`) are **rationed** ‚Äî at most one strong coloured action per view, beside the single teal primary. Only status actions hover in-hue; neutral buttons (`outline` / `secondary` / `ghost`) hover grey *by design* ‚Äî that's correct, not a bug.
- Status ‚Üí muted **pill** chips (rounded-full), AA text. Plain labels/metadata ‚Üí neutral chips.
- Cards default **flat** (hairline); use raised only to lift something genuinely above the page.
- Charts ‚Üí categorical palette; highlight the current / most-relevant series.
- Forms ‚Üí label above field, hint & error in `caption`; error uses `destructive` **plus text** (never colour alone).

## 6 ¬∑ Do / don't
| ‚úÖ Do | ‚ùå Don't |
|---|---|
| `New case` | `NEW CASE` ¬∑ `New Case` |
| a ghost hover you can see on a cheap screen | a hover that vanishes into the background |
| `‚Üì 3.1%` in red **with the arrow** | red text with no arrow |
| one teal primary action per view | teal sprinkled as decoration |
| roomy rows, quiet headers | a dense grid that reads like a spreadsheet |

---

*Source of design intent. `llms.txt` and `CLAUDE.md` reference this file, and it ships as a registry item ‚Äî so any project or AI building with Pucar inherits these rules.*

# Screen craft ‚Äî how to compose a screen that passes a professional eye > `design-guidelines.md` is the **law** (what and why). This file is the **craft** (how). > Read BOTH before building any screen. The law without the craft produces on-brand screens with amateur bones ‚Äî wrong spacing, mushy hierarchy, missing states. This file exists because that happened. The rule underneath everything here: **never freestyle layout.** Start from a scaffold (¬ß2), take every gap from the ladder (¬ß1), run the review pass (¬ß8) before calling anything done. --- ## 0 ¬∑ Before you place anything Answer these in one line each ‚Äî in a comment or to yourself. If you can't, stop and ask: 1. **The job:** what does the user come to this screen to do? One sentence. 2. **The one action:** which single button is THE primary action? (Everything else is outline/ghost/link.) 3. **The mode:** citizen (stressed, occasional, mobile-likely ‚Üí generous, one decision at a time) or staff (trained, all-day, data-dense ‚Üí earned density)? This sets widths, spacing, and target sizes for the whole screen (¬ß7). 4. **The hierarchy:** what must be seen 1st, 2nd, 3rd? If everything feels important, nothing is ‚Äî cut or demote. ## 1 ¬∑ Spacing ‚Äî proximity is meaning Space is not decoration; it *is* the grouping structure. Related things sit closer than unrelated things ‚Äî that single principle produces most of "professional". **The ladder ‚Äî these gaps only.** Tailwind: `1=4px 2=8px 3=12px 4=16px 6=24px 8=32px 12=48px 16=64px`. Never arbitrary values (`p-[13px]`, `mt-5`, `gap-7` are all bugs). If a design feels like it needs an in-between value, the grouping is wrong, not the ladder. **Micro-spacing addendum (ruling 2026-07-21):** *inside* a single control or chip ‚Äî icon‚Üîlabel, chip padding, track inset ‚Äî the half-steps `0.5=2px 1.5=6px 2.5=10px` are legal, because text metrics need them. They are **never** legal *between* elements: layout gaps come only from the ladder above. (`gap-1.5` inside a button: fine. `space-y-1.5` between a title and subtitle: fine, that pair is one lockup. `gap-2.5` between two cards: bug.) **Distance encodes relationship:** | Relationship | Gap | |---|---| | Inside an element (icon ‚Üî its text) | `gap-1.5` / `gap-2` | | Label ‚Üî its field ¬∑ title ‚Üî its subtitle | `gap-1.5` / `gap-2` | | Field ‚Üî next field in the same group | `gap-4` ‚Ä¶ `gap-6` | | Card ‚Üî card ¬∑ group ‚Üî group | `gap-6` | | Section ‚Üî section on a page | `space-y-8` (staff) / `space-y-10`‚Äì`12` (citizen) | | Page header ‚Üî first content | `mb-8` | **Container padding:** cards `p-6` (never less than the largest gap inside them); page gutters `px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8`, vertical `py-8`; dialog/sheet content `p-6`. **Three checks that catch 90% of spacing bugs:** - **Padding ‚â• gap** ‚Äî a container's padding is never smaller than the gaps inside it (otherwise content hugs the edge while floating apart internally). - **Outside > inside** ‚Äî the space *around* a group is always larger than the spaces *within* it. If `gap` between groups ‚â§ `gap` within groups, grouping is illegible. - **Symmetry** ‚Äî left/right padding equal; the same section rhythm all the way down the page. One inconsistent margin reads as a mistake everywhere. When something feels cramped, add space **between groups**, not inside elements. ## 2 ¬∑ Scaffolds ‚Äî start from a skeleton, never a blank file Pick the matching skeleton and fill it. These encode the decisions that keep screens consistent. **Every page:** ```tsx
{/* header block */}

Hearings today

{/* citizen pages: text-title */}

Supporting line ‚Äî what this screen is, in one sentence.

{/* THE primary action ‚Äî exactly one, teal */}
{/* sections */}
``` Widths: staff/table pages `max-w-6xl`‚Äì`7xl` ¬∑ citizen flows and forms `max-w-2xl`‚Äì`3xl` ¬∑ reading content `max-w-prose`. Never stretch a form to fill a wide screen. **Form screen** (`max-w-2xl`): sections as cards or `space-y-10` groups. Each section = `title-s` heading + optional `caption` muted description + fields in `space-y-6`. **Single column always** ‚Äî two-column forms cause skipped fields and broken tab order; the only exception is tightly-coupled pairs (city/PIN) as `grid grid-cols-2 gap-4`. Label above field (`gap-2`); hint/error in `caption` under the field. Actions in a row at the end: `
` ‚Äî ghost/outline secondary first, primary last (outer position). Long legal forms: end with a **review step**, not a surprise submit. **List/table screen:** toolbar (`flex items-center justify-between gap-4 mb-6`: search left ‚Äî labeled, `sr-only` is fine; filters middle; primary action right) ‚Üí table in a flat card ‚Üí footer with count + pagination. Row anatomy: one primary line (`font-medium`) + one muted secondary line (CNR in `font-mono text-muted-foreground`); headers quiet (`text-muted-foreground`, sentence case); row actions as a ghost `‚Ä¶` menu at row end; whole row clickable to detail. **Detail screen:** identity header (title + status pill inline, muted mono meta line below, actions right) ‚Üí `grid gap-6 lg:grid-cols-3`: main content `lg:col-span-2`, aside (parties, dates, documents) in the third. Sections inside use the same `space-y-8`. **Dashboard:** stat row `grid gap-6 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4`; each stat card = `caption` muted label ‚Üí number in `text-title tabular-nums` ‚Üí delta with **arrow + colour** (never colour alone). Then chart + list pairings, each in a card with a quiet `title-s` header row. **Empty state (its own component shape, centered ‚Äî the one place centering is right):** ```tsx

No cases yet

When you file a complaint, it appears here with its hearing dates.

``` ## 3 ¬∑ Hierarchy ‚Äî the squint test - **One `

` per screen.** The ladder: page title `title-l` (staff) / `title` (citizen) ‚Üí section `title-s` ‚Üí card/group title `text-body font-medium` ‚Üí content `body` / `body-compact` ‚Üí metadata `caption text-muted-foreground`. Don't invent intermediate sizes; don't skip levels. - **At most 3 levels of emphasis visible at once.** Emphasis comes from **weight (500/600) and muting (`text-muted-foreground`)** ‚Äî never from size sprawl or colour (law: hierarchy = weight + neutral-11). - **Elevate the one by muting the many.** Labels, IDs, timestamps, helper text ‚Üí muted. The *data* is the figure; everything else is ground. If a row/card has two bold things, one of them is wrong. - Numbers align in columns: `tabular-nums`. Identifiers (CNR, case no.): `font-mono text-muted-foreground`. - **The squint test:** blur your eyes at the rendered screen. You should still find: the page title, the primary action, and the first row/card of real content. If anything else competes, demote it. ## 4 ¬∑ Alignment & shape - **One left spine.** Page title, section headings, card contents, and form labels all start at the same x-position. Mixed indents read as sloppiness even when nobody can name why. - **Center only** empty states and single-purpose confirmation moments. Never center body text or form labels. - Icon + text: `flex items-center gap-2`, icon `size-4` matched to line-height. Buttons in a row share ONE size. - Inputs in a group share one width; never a ragged right edge of mixed-width fields. - Radius and elevation are semantic (law ¬ßElevation): cards flat-with-hairline by default, `shadow-raised` only for genuine lift, overlays/modals use their own levels. No decorative shadows, no mixed radii. ## 5 ¬∑ UX patterns ‚Äî when to reach for what **Dialog vs sheet vs page:** dialog = one decision or a tiny form (‚â§ ~4 fields); sheet = contextual create/edit while the list stays visible behind; full page = anything multi-section, anything with legal weight (filings are pages, not popups). Destructive confirms = `alert-dialog`, always naming the object and the consequence ("Delete draft complaint against Suresh Kumar? This can't be undone.") with `destructive-solid` confirm. **Feedback:** background/async results ‚Üí toast (sonner). Blocking context the user must read ‚Üí inline `Alert` above the content it blocks. Field problems ‚Üí inline error under the field (`caption` + destructive + text, never colour alone). Never a toast for a field error; never an alert for a success. **Filtering & search:** active filters render as removable chips under the toolbar; always show a result count ("14 cases"); filtered-empty gets its own empty state with "Clear filters", distinct from true-empty. **Progressive disclosure:** show the essential, link the rest ("4 of 27 shown ‚Üí View cause list"). Rare/advanced form fields behind a collapsible, not deleted. Critical info never lives *only* in a tooltip or hover. **Status & time:** state ‚Üí muted pill; case progress ‚Üí vertical timeline (past = muted dot, current = brand, future = hollow); neutral metadata ‚Üí plain muted text, not a pill. Pill inflation is real ‚Äî if everything has a pill, nothing has status. **Selection density:** ‚â§5 options ‚Üí radio group / toggle group (visible, one tap); >5 ‚Üí select; multi-select from many ‚Üí checkboxes in a scroll area, chosen items echoed as chips. **Loading:** skeletons that **mirror the final layout** (same heights, same grid ‚Äî no layout jump), never a bare centered spinner for a whole page. Buttons show in-button pending state on submit. ## 6 ¬∑ A screen is five screens ‚Äî states are the design Nothing is "done" with only the happy path. Every screen ships with: 1. **Default** ‚Äî real-shaped data, not lorem ipsum (use plausible Kerala names, real CNR formats). 2. **Empty** ‚Äî first-run teaches the first action (¬ß2 recipe). Filtered-empty is a separate, lighter state. 3. **Loading** ‚Äî skeleton mirroring layout. 4. **Error** ‚Äî what happened + what to do now, in plain sentences ("Couldn't load today's hearings. Check your connection and retry."). Never a bare code. 5. **Edge** ‚Äî the longest party name you can imagine (and its Malayalam version), a 16-char CNR, 0 items, 1 item, 10,000 items, tomorrow's date, a hearing 4 years out. Truncate deliberately (`truncate` + full value on hover/detail), wrap intentionally, and make sure the layout survives all of it. ## 7 ¬∑ Density modes (from ¬ß0's answer) - **Citizen:** `max-w-2xl/3xl` ¬∑ targets ‚â• 44px (`size="lg"` for primary flows) ¬∑ `body` 16 everywhere ¬∑ `space-y-10`+ between sections ¬∑ one decision per screen where possible ¬∑ progress indicator on multi-step flows. - **Staff:** density earned in **tables only**, via row padding and line-height ‚Äî never by shrinking type below `body-compact` (14) or targets below 40px. Page furniture (headers, toolbars) keeps the generous rhythm even on dense screens. ## 8 ¬∑ The review pass ‚Äî run this before declaring done Go through literally, against the rendered screen (light AND dark, 375/768/1280): - [ ] Every gap/padding is from the ladder ‚Äî zero arbitrary values in the diff - [ ] Padding ‚â• gap in every container; outside > inside for every group - [ ] Exactly one primary (teal) action; everything else outline/ghost/link - [ ] Exactly one `

`; type ladder with no skips; ‚â§3 emphasis levels per view - [ ] Squint test: title, primary action, first content ‚Äî findable blurred - [ ] One left spine; forms single-column at `max-w-2xl`; no full-width inputs on wide screens - [ ] All five states exist (default/empty/loading/error/edge) and the layout survives the edge kit - [ ] Status has icon-or-text with its colour; deltas have arrows; sentence case everywhere - [ ] Focus visible on every interactive element by keyboard-tabbing through - [ ] Dark mode actually checked, not assumed If any box fails, fix it before showing the screen. This pass is not optional polish ‚Äî it's the difference this file exists to make. --- *Companion to `design-guidelines.md` (the law) and `CLAUDE.md` (the workflow). Ships with the repo so every human and AI designing with Pucar composes screens the same way.*

# Component usage ‚Äî when and how to use what

> The third law doc. `design-guidelines.md` says what the system looks like; `screen-craft.md` says how screens compose; **this file says which component to reach for, and when not to.** Format follows the systems built for the same job as ours ‚Äî GOV.UK ("when to use / when not to use"), USWDS ("when to consider something else"), Carbon (feedback decision rules).
>
> Every `components/ui/*` file carries a distilled version of its entry as a top comment ‚Äî agents and humans read those in place. This file is the full law; if they disagree, this file wins and the comment is a bug.

**The grammar of every entry:** *Job* (one sentence) ¬∑ *Use when* ¬∑ *Don't use* (with the "‚Üí use X instead" pointer) ¬∑ *Variants* (what each means, not just what exists). Rationing and accessibility notes where the law demands them.

---

## 1 ¬∑ Actions

**The decision:** does it *do* something (button) or *go* somewhere (link)? Navigation between pages is a link (`text-primary`, underline on hover) ‚Äî buttons are for actions. USWDS's rule: lead with a verb; less important actions get quieter variants, not more buttons.

**The emphasis ladder ‚Äî exactly one primary per view:** `default` (teal, THE action) ‚Üí `secondary` (grey fill, supporting) ‚Üí `outline` (border, parallel choices) ‚Üí `ghost` (bare, repeated/row-level) ‚Üí `link` (inline reference). Status solids (`success/warning/info/destructive-solid`) sit beside ‚Äî not above ‚Äî the primary, rationed to one strong coloured action per view.

### button
- **Job.** Trigger an action, in one press.
- **Use when** the user commits, submits, opens, confirms, files.
- **Don't use** for navigation between pages ‚Üí use a link; for switching an on/off state ‚Üí `switch` or `toggle`; for choosing one of ‚â§5 options ‚Üí `toggle-group` or `radio-group`.
- **Variants.** One `default` (teal) per view ‚Äî more than one main call to action makes it harder to know what to do next (GOV.UK). `destructive` (tinted) for row-level dangerous actions; it escalates to solid red on hover ‚Äî that's the design, not a bug. `destructive-solid` only as the confirm inside an `alert-dialog`. `warning`/`success`/`info` solids: at most one, only when the action *is* that status ("Approve filing").
- **Disabled buttons are a last resort** ‚Äî they have poor contrast and confuse users (GOV.UK). Prefer keeping the button active and showing the inline error on press.
- **Labels:** sentence case, verb first, ‚â§3 words ("File a complaint", never "Complaint filing" or "SUBMIT"). In-progress: swap label for `spinner` + "Filing‚Ä¶" inside the same button.

### button-group
- **Job.** One decision expressed as a segmented cluster (pagination arrows, split button, view switch).
- **Use when** the actions are variations of one choice and belong under one visual boundary.
- **Don't use** to park several unrelated primaries side by side ‚Üí lay out separate buttons with `gap-3`; for one-of-N selection that persists ‚Üí `toggle-group`.

### toggle ¬∑ toggle-group
- **Job.** A pressed/unpressed state (`toggle`) or a persistent one-of-N / many-of-N choice (`toggle-group`).
- **Use when** ‚â§5 options should stay visible and one-tap reachable (density picker, view switch, formatting bar).
- **Don't use** for >5 options ‚Üí `select`; for an on/off setting with a sentence label ‚Üí `switch`; for form data that gets submitted ‚Üí `radio-group`/`checkbox` (they carry form semantics; toggles are UI state).
- **The chosen state carries the brand tint** ‚Äî grey is hover-only (grey selection reads as disabled; DECISIONS 2026-07-21).

---

## 2 ¬∑ Status & feedback

**The decision tree (Carbon's split, adapted):**
- Known *before* the page renders, can't be dismissed ‚Üí **banner region** (compose with `bg-info-muted`, as scrutiny does) ‚Äî it loads with the page and is information, not feedback.
- Result of a user/system action, tied to *this section* of the page, must be read ‚Üí **alert** (inline, persistent until resolved).
- Result of a background/async action, tied to *no particular section* ‚Üí **toast** (`sonner`; auto-dismisses, so never put anything the user must act on in one).
- A problem with *one field* ‚Üí inline `field` error under that field. Never a toast for a field error; never an alert for a success.
- The *state of a thing* (case, fee, document) ‚Üí **badge**. The *progress of a process* ‚Üí **progress**. The *absence of things* ‚Üí **empty**.

### alert
- **Job.** Blocking, contextual information the user must read before continuing with the section below it.
- **Use when** an error/warning/notice relates to the content it sits above, and must persist until resolved.
- **Don't use** for transient success ("Saved") ‚Üí toast; for page-level standing notices ‚Üí banner region; for one field's problem ‚Üí `field` error.
- **Variants.** `default` (neutral notice) and `destructive` (the title and icon carry the status ink; the description stays muted ‚Äî a wall of red text shouts).

### sonner (toast)
- **Job.** Non-blocking confirmation of a background or just-completed action.
- **Use when** the user's flow shouldn't stop: "Draft saved", "Document uploaded", "Copied".
- **Don't use** for anything requiring action or that the user must not miss ‚Äî toasts auto-dismiss in seconds and screen-reader users may never hear them ‚Üí alert or dialog; never for validation errors.

### badge
- **Job.** THE chip. A short status or category label on an object.
- **Use when** a row/card needs its state readable at a glance: `success` ("Fee paid"), `warning` ("Awaiting docs"), `info` ("Under hearing"), `destructive` ("Dishonoured").
- **Don't use** status variants for decoration or emphasis ‚Äî status colours mean status; for neutral metadata ("4 documents") ‚Üí `secondary`/`outline`, or plain muted text. **Pill inflation is real: if everything has a badge, nothing has status.**
- Pair the colour with words ‚Äî never colour alone.

### progress
- **Job.** How far a determinate process has got (upload, multi-step filing).
- **Don't use** for indeterminate waits ‚Üí `spinner` (in context) or `skeleton` (whole regions); not as a chart ‚Üí `chart`.

### spinner
- **Job.** In-context pending state: inside a pressed button, beside a refreshing row.
- **Don't use** as a bare centered spinner for a whole page ‚Äî full-page loading is a `skeleton` that mirrors the final layout (no layout jump when content lands).

### skeleton
- **Job.** Loading placeholder that mirrors the final layout ‚Äî same heights, same grid.
- **Use when** a page or region is fetching its initial data.
- **Don't use** shapeless grey boxes that don't match what will render ‚Äî the mirror is the point.

### empty
- **Job.** The canonical empty state: icon, one-line title, one supportive sentence, the first action.
- **Use when** a list/region has no items yet (teach the first action) ‚Äî and use a *lighter* variant for filtered-empty ("No cases match ‚Äî clear filters"), which is a different state from true-empty.
- **Don't use** for errors ‚Üí alert with a retry; never leave a region silently blank.

---

## 3 ¬∑ Forms

**The frame:** every field = `field` (label above, hint below label, error below control ‚Äî caption size, destructive ink *plus text*, never colour alone). Single column, `max-w-2xl`. Visible labels always ‚Äî placeholder-only fields fail accessibility (and our audit).

### field ¬∑ label
- **Job.** The form anatomy wrapper: spacing, aria wiring, error slots.
- **Use when** building ANY form control group ‚Äî it makes label-less fields hard to write.
- **Don't** hand-compose label+input+error with ad-hoc spacing ‚Üí this is what field is for.

### input
- **Job.** Single-line free text.
- **Don't use** for long text ‚Üí `textarea`; for constrained choices ‚Üí `select`/`radio-group`; for text with affixes or inline actions ‚Üí `input-group`.
- Machine-prefilled, human-unverified values get `data-prefilled` (amber fill) until the user edits or confirms.

### textarea
- **Job.** Multi-line free text (grounds of complaint, remarks).
- Give it a visible character expectation if one exists; grows with content, never scrolls invisibly at 2 lines.

### input-group
- **Job.** A field with affixes: search icon, ‚Çπ prefix, unit suffix, copy button.
- **Don't use** to decorate a plain field ‚Äî if the affix carries no meaning, use `input`.

### input-otp
- **Job.** Fixed-length verification code (e-filing OTP). Monospaced, grouped, 40px targets.
- **Don't use** for anything that isn't a code ‚Äî it's not a styled input.

### select
- **Job.** One choice from >5 options where showing all would crowd the screen.
- **Don't use** for ‚â§5 options ‚Üí `radio-group`/`toggle-group` (visible options, one tap ‚Äî hiding 3 choices behind a dropdown is pure friction); for one-of-many with search (party names, police stations) ‚Üí command-in-popover (combobox pattern).

### checkbox
- **Job.** Independent yes/no declarations, or many-of-N selection.
- **Use when** each option stands alone ("Apply for exemption‚Ä¶"); >7 options ‚Üí checkboxes inside a `scroll-area` with chosen items echoed as chips.
- **Don't use** for mutually exclusive options ‚Üí `radio-group`; for instant-effect settings ‚Üí `switch`.

### radio-group
- **Job.** Exactly one of 2‚Äì5 visible, submitted options.
- **Don't use** when unselected isn't a valid transit state or the effect is instant ‚Üí `switch`/`toggle-group`; >5 options ‚Üí `select`.

### switch
- **Job.** An instant on/off setting ‚Äî flipping it takes effect now, no submit.
- **Don't use** inside a submitted form (state is ambiguous until save) ‚Üí `checkbox`.

### slider
- **Job.** Choosing a value from a continuous/dense range where the *relative* position matters.
- **Don't use** when the exact number matters (amounts, dates) ‚Üí `input` with `inputMode="numeric"` ‚Äî sliders are hostile to precision and to screen readers for exact entry.

### calendar
- **Job.** Date choice where the *week context* matters (hearings, adjournments).
- **Don't use** for known dates the user can type faster (date of birth) ‚Üí segmented text inputs (GOV.UK memorable-dates pattern).
- Week starts Sunday, deterministic; selected day carries the brand tint; today is marked without colour alone.

### document-slot
- **Job.** The e-filing upload anatomy: filled (sunken well + thumbnail + quality pill), empty (dashed boundary + required mark), processing.
- **Don't** rebuild ad-hoc upload rows ‚Äî this is the one upload shape.

---

## 4 ¬∑ Containers & structure

**The decision:** page-level grouping ‚Üí `card` (one hairline, flat). Grouping *inside* a card ‚Üí `surface-sunken` well (fill, no border ‚Äî box-in-box reads amateur). Ephemeral grouping ‚Üí whitespace alone (the ladder groups harder than boxes do). Key‚Äìvalue facts ‚Üí `description-list`. Rows of records ‚Üí `table`.

### card
- **Job.** A bounded region of related content on the page ‚Äî one hairline, flat by default.
- **Don't** nest cards ‚Üí inner grouping is a `surface-sunken` well; don't reach for `shadow-raised` unless the thing genuinely floats above the page (menus float; summaries don't).
- **Variants.** `size="sm"` (16px padding) only for staff-dense grids.

### description-list
- **Job.** Label‚Äìvalue pairs: case metadata, review summaries, detail asides.
- **Don't use** a two-column `table` for key‚Äìvalue data ‚Üí tables are for *many records*, description lists are for *one record's facts*.

### table
- **Job.** Records √ó attributes, for trained staff.
- **Use when** users scan/compare many rows; one primary line per row, identifiers in mono muted, headers quiet (the data reads first, headers second).
- **Don't use** for layout, ever; on mobile it scrolls inside its own container ‚Äî never crushes columns.

### separator
- **Job.** A quiet hairline between meta items (inline) or blocks (horizontal).
- **Don't** stack separators to fake structure ‚Üí if you need more than dividers, the grouping wants a well or a card. Prefer dividers over density.

### scroll-area
- **Job.** A bounded, styled scroll region for long option lists (acts invoked, multi-select).
- **Don't use** to make a page section scroll independently without a reason ‚Äî nested scroll on touch is a trap.

### accordion
- **Job.** One-at-a-time expandable Q&A / help content (citizen-facing FAQs).
- **Don't use** to hide critical filing information the user *must* see ‚Üí put it in the page; for independent sections that can all be open ‚Üí `collapsible` per section.

### collapsible
- **Job.** Progressive disclosure of rare/advanced options ‚Äî hidden, never deleted.
- **Don't** put anything *required* behind one; critical info never lives only behind a disclosure.

---

## 5 ¬∑ Overlays

**The decision (screen-craft ¬ß5, sharpened):** one decision or ‚â§4 fields ‚Üí `dialog`. Destructive confirm ‚Üí `alert-dialog`, always naming the object and consequence. Contextual create/edit while the list stays visible ‚Üí `sheet` (desktop side panel). Touch bottom-sheet ‚Üí `drawer`. Anything multi-section or with legal weight ‚Üí a full page; **filings are pages, not popups.** Small anchored choices ‚Üí `popover`/`dropdown-menu`. Labels for icon buttons ‚Üí `tooltip`. Rich preview on hover ‚Üí `hover-card` (desktop-only affordance ‚Äî never the sole path to anything).

### dialog
- **Job.** One focused decision or a tiny form, blocking the page.
- **Don't use** for multi-section content or long forms ‚Üí full page; for destructive confirms ‚Üí `alert-dialog`; opening a dialog from a dialog is a design failure.

### alert-dialog
- **Job.** Confirm a destructive or irreversible act.
- **The confirm names the object and consequence** ("Withdraw the complaint against Suresh Kumar? ‚Ä¶ can't be undone") ‚Äî never a bare "Are you sure?". Confirm button is `destructive-solid`; cancel is the safe default and phrased as the safe action ("Keep the draft").

### sheet
- **Job.** A side panel for contextual create/edit/detail while the underlying list stays visible.
- **Don't use** for confirmations ‚Üí dialog; below `md` prefer `drawer` (thumb-reachable).

### drawer
- **Job.** The touch bottom-sheet ‚Äî mobile counterpart to sheet/dialog for citizen flows.
- **Don't use** on desktop as a novelty ‚Üí sheet.

### popover
- **Job.** Small anchored surface with interactive content (filters, a mini form, a picker).
- **Don't use** for plain-text labels ‚Üí `tooltip`; for a list of actions ‚Üí `dropdown-menu`; anything a user must complete ‚Üí dialog (popovers dismiss too easily).

### dropdown-menu
- **Job.** Actions on the thing you clicked (row `‚Ä¶` menu, header account menu).
- **Don't use** for choosing form data ‚Üí `select` (form semantics, typeahead); destructive items use the ink + tint treatment and sit last, separated.

### menubar
- **Job.** Desktop application-style command surface (document workspace: File/View/Help).
- **Don't use** for site navigation ‚Üí `navigation-menu` or the sidebar. Staff-only; citizens never need a menubar.

### tooltip
- **Job.** A short text label for an icon-only control, on hover/focus.
- **Critical information never lives only in a tooltip** ‚Äî touch users and screen-reader flows may never see it. Icon-only buttons still need `aria-label` (the tooltip is a courtesy, not the name).

### hover-card
- **Job.** Rich preview of a linked entity (party, case) on hover ‚Äî desktop enhancement.
- **Don't** gate any task on it; there must always be a click-through path.

### command
- **Job.** The staff jump-to palette (‚åòK): fuzzy search across cases, parties, actions.
- **Don't use** as a general search results page ‚Üí that's a list screen; citizens get visible search fields, not keyboard palettes.

---

## 6 ¬∑ Navigation

**The decision:** where am I in the *app* ‚Üí `sidebar` (workspace) / `navigation-menu` (public site). Where am I in the *hierarchy* ‚Üí `breadcrumb`. Switching *views of one thing* ‚Üí `tabs`. Moving through *pages of a list* ‚Üí `pagination`.

### tabs
- **Job.** Parallel views of the same object (Overview / Documents / Orders on one case).
- **Don't use** for sequential steps ‚Üí stepper pattern (multi-step flows are ordered; tabs imply free order); don't nest tabs; >5 tabs means the object wants a different layout.

### breadcrumb
- **Job.** Ancestry trail on detail pages ‚Äî where this page sits, one click up.
- **Don't use** on top-level pages (nothing above them) or as a step indicator.

### pagination
- **Job.** Page-through for long lists, with the count visible ("Page 4 of 12").
- **Don't use** infinite scroll for records with legal weight ‚Äî people need stable positions they can return to and cite.

### navigation-menu
- **Job.** Top-level navigation for public/citizen surfaces without the workspace sidebar.
- **Don't use** inside the workspace (the sidebar owns that) ‚Äî two competing navigations disorient.

### sidebar
- **Job.** The workspace navigation rail (staff surfaces). Selection = brand tint; hover = grey.
- Collapses to a sheet drawer below `md` ‚Äî never simply vanishes.

---

## 7 ¬∑ Identity & data

### avatar
- **Job.** A person, as image or monogram. Monogram tiles use the `brand-muted` pair.
- **Don't use** for organisations/objects ‚Üí an icon tile; never as decoration.

### chart
- **Job.** Data visualisation on the categorical palette (`chart-1‚Ä¶5`) ‚Äî chosen for mutual distinction.
- **Charts never borrow status colours** ‚Äî a red line means "series 3", not "bad", or it means nothing. Highlight the current/most-relevant series; legends and tooltips carry labels so colour is never alone.

---

## 8 ¬∑ Motion (the law lives in design-guidelines ¬ßMotion)

Micro feedback (hover, focus, pressed) **100‚Äì150ms** ¬∑ surface transitions (overlays, expansion) **200‚Äì250ms** ¬∑ nothing over **300ms**, ever ‚Äî this is a tool, not a show. Ease-out entering, ease-in exiting (Material's decelerate/accelerate rule). Motion is *feedback for something the user did* ‚Äî never ambient, never decorative. `prefers-reduced-motion` collapses all of it to instant state changes, globally.

---

*Companion to `design-guidelines.md` (the law) and `screen-craft.md` (the craft). Ships with `design-rules` in the registry, so consumers inherit the judgment, not just the components.*

# Designing with Pucar ¬∑ ON Court

You are building for a courts product used by **citizens under stress** and **staff all day**. The register is calm, dignified public service ‚Äî premium through restraint. When unsure, choose the calmer, more legible, more forgiving option.

## Before anything: read the law

In this repo (installed via the `design-rules` registry item):

1. **`design-guidelines.md`** ‚Äî the law. Non-negotiables: sentence case only; brand teal rationed to primary/active/focus; status colours mean status; three treatments per status hue (solid fill / opaque tint pair / ink ‚Äî solids never as text, alpha status fills banned); the monotone grey ladder (interaction moves one step down); control metrics (40px fields, radius by role, 24px container padding); WCAG 2.2 AA floor; never colour alone; motion ‚â§300ms, feedback-only.
2. **`screen-craft.md`** ‚Äî the craft. Spacing ladder (4/8/12/16/24/32/48/64; micro 2/6/10 inside controls only), page scaffolds, hierarchy/squint test, the five screen states, citizen vs staff density, the ¬ß8 review pass.
3. **`component-usage.md`** ‚Äî when and how to use each component, with the "use X instead" pointers and four decision trees (actions, feedback, containers, overlays, navigation).

**Never freestyle layout.** Start from a screen-craft scaffold; take every gap from the ladder.

## Hard rules that generated UI most often breaks

- Exactly **one** teal primary action per view; status solids rationed to one.
- **Tokens, never hardcoded** ‚Äî `bg-primary`, `text-body`, `text-success-ink`, `bg-surface-sunken`; no raw hex, no `neutral-N`, no `text-sm`, no bare `rounded`, no alpha status tints.
- Status chip = `Badge` (the one chip). Status text/icons on neutral = `*-ink`. Status colours never used to decorate.
- Fields default 40px; container padding 24; forms single-column at `max-w-2xl`; label above field, always visible.
- Every screen ships five states: default (court-shaped data ‚Äî Kerala names, real CNR formats), empty, loading (skeletons that mirror layout), error (plain sentences + what to do), edge (longest names, 16-char CNRs, 0/1/10,000 items).
- Feedback routing: field problem ‚Üí inline field error ¬∑ section-blocking ‚Üí alert ¬∑ background result ‚Üí toast ¬∑ standing notice ‚Üí banner region. Never a toast for a field error.
- Overlays: one decision ‚Üí dialog ¬∑ destructive confirm ‚Üí alert-dialog naming object + consequence ¬∑ side context ‚Üí sheet (drawer on touch) ¬∑ anything multi-section or legally weighty ‚Üí full page.

## Verify before declaring done

Run screen-craft's ¬ß8 review pass literally: ladder-only gaps, one h1, squint test, one left spine, five states, focus visible by keyboard, **light AND dark checked at 375/768/1280**. Text ‚â•4.5:1, boundaries ‚â•3:1 ‚Äî in this repo `pnpm gate` proves token pairs; anything hand-composed you verify yourself.

## Machine index

`llms.txt` (repo root or `https://pucar-ui.vercel.app/llms.txt`) lists every token and installable component. The live gallery (`/`) and measured token lab (`/foundations`) are the review surfaces.
